title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Frankie and Roger discuss their delayed pursuit of the Kemble couple, though Frankie privately notes that their original intention had been to pursue Roger himself. Their conversation shifts to Moira Nicholson, whom Frankie perceives as a helpless and terrified girl. Roger expresses his disdain for such passivity, arguing that a person with true courage would fight or kill to survive rather than remain a victim. Frankie reflects on her own dislike of Moira and her annoyance regarding Bobby’s interest in her, specifically his obsession with a particular photograph.

The discussion turns to the treatment of Henry Baskington-French. Roger initially doubts the necessity of sending Henry to Granchester, noting that Henry's consent is required. However, Sylvia Baskerville-French approaches them and reveals that Dr. Nicholson has told her the truth about Henry's addiction. Sylvia announces that Henry has already agreed to go to Granchester the following day to be under the doctor's care.

Roger and Frankie both protest this decision; Roger argues that Henry should seek treatment elsewhere to avoid remaining close to home. Sylvia, however, has undergone a complete change of heart regarding Dr. Nicholson. Despite her previous biases, she now views him as kind, generous, and intelligent. She becomes angry with Roger for his inconsistency and accuses Frankie of wanting Henry to be far away from her. Although Frankie suggests that Dr. Nicholson might be a quack, Sylvia remains a staunch supporter of the doctor. Roger eventually leaves the group to leave a message for Dr. Nicholson, a sudden eagerness that leaves Sylvia confused.
